decisions.decision: PVC accessModes default stays [ReadWriteMany] in production; test sets [ReadWriteOnce] via --set helm flag
decisions.rationale: Production spec intent is RWX (shared workspace state across Pods); kind's local-path provisioner only supports RWO; override stays test-local to honor the production contract.

Plan-check, then execute Wave 5 via `/gsd-execute-phase 02.2 --wave 5 --auto --no-transition`. If APPROVED gate fires, Plan 02.2-02 closeout activates in Wave 6.
context_notes: Phase 02.2 started as a 4-fix tactical phase to close Phase 02.1's BLOCKED runtime gate. Reality has been a cascade of 4 latent bugs each masked by the previous one: (1) source-shape timing fixes unmasked PVC RWX/RWO mismatch, (2) PVC fix unmasked --metrics-bind-address undefined, (3) metrics fix unmasked --webhook-cert-path undefined, (4) webhook fix unmasked Makefile test-budget too tight. The flag-mismatch cascade chain (cascades 2-3) terminated cleanly at 5/5 chart args defined. Cascade-4 is structurally different — fitness, not correctness; specs ARE running but go test is SIGKILLed before Ginkgo flushes. Manager Pod is now Running 0 restarts for 11+ min spanning clean run + rerun. PVC fix preserved (Bound RWO). All flag fixes preserved (no flag-not-defined errors). User explicitly chose tactical iteration over audit-everything 3x, and the choice has been validated by cascade-4 being non-predictable from a static audit. Recommended next step is a single-line Makefile edit (timeout 300s → 600s). If that bumps work, Phase 02.2 finally closes and Plan 02.2-02 closeout activates.
